symptoms.symptom_name: Epigastric pain boring through to the back

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic radiation pattern reflecting the pancreas's retroperitoneal location.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain relieved by leaning forward, worse lying flat

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Highly specific postural feature for pancreatic inflammation.

symptoms.symptom_name: More than 10 episodes of vomiting with complete fluid intolerance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Indicates significant ongoing fluid losses and reactive ileus, raising risk of dehydration and pre-renal injury.

symptoms.symptom_name: Weakness, lightheadedness, and overnight subjective fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic signs consistent with evolving hypovolaemia and/or SIRS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent heavy alcohol consumption preceding onset

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Alcohol is one of the two most common causes of acute pancreatitis (with gallstones).

symptoms.symptom_name: Localised epigastric tenderness with voluntary guarding and absent bowel sounds on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Consistent with pancreatic inflammation and reactive ileus; clinician-only finding sealed from telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum lipase or amylase elevated greater than three times the upper limit of normal

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated greater than three times the upper limit of normal, confirming acute pancreatitis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis unconfirmed; telehealth cannot obtain this and should recommend it occur as part of emergency assessment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: One of the two required diagnostic criteria (with clinical presentation) per standard diagnostic definitions of acute pancreatitis.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Epigastric-to-back pain relieved by leaning forward, profuse vomiting with fluid intolerance, and a recent alcohol trigger together constitute a classic pancreatitis presentation with early signs of hypovolaemia, mandating urgent same-day emergency department assessment for IV fluid resuscitation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum lipase and full metabolic panel (renal function, calcium, glucose, LFTs)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the diagnosis and assesses severity/complications; cannot be performed via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: IV fluid resuscitation and antiemetic therapy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive early management for hypovolaemia and intractable vomiting; requires in-person administration.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT abdomen as a routine first step
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not required for initial diagnosis (which is clinical plus lipase); CT is reserved for severity grading or when the diagnosis is unclear, and should not delay fluid resuscitation.
